Clopidogrel (Danielak 2017)

Source: vignettes/articles/Danielak_2017_clopidogrel.Rmd
Danielak_2017_clopidogrel.Rmd

Model and source

  • Citation: Danielak D, Karazniewicz-Lada M, Komosa A, Burchardt P, Lesiak M, Kruszyna L, Graczyk-Szuster A, Glowka F. Influence of genetic co-factors on the population pharmacokinetic model for clopidogrel and its active thiol metabolite. Eur J Clin Pharmacol. 2017;73(12):1623-1632. doi:10.1007/s00228-017-2334-z
  • Description: Joint parent-metabolite population PK model for oral clopidogrel and its active thiol H4 metabolite (the antiplatelet-active diastereomer) in adult Caucasian patients undergoing elective coronarography or percutaneous coronary intervention on chronic clopidogrel 75 mg/day (Danielak 2017). Clopidogrel is described by a one-compartment model with first-order absorption (rate constant ka = source k12) and first-order elimination (CL/F = source CL/F, V/F = source V2/F). The H4 metabolite is described by a one-compartment model with irreversible first-order formation from clopidogrel central at the rate FM * CL/F * (clopidogrel central / Vc) and first-order elimination (CL_h4/F = source Q2/F, V_h4/F = source V3/F). FM was constrained to <= 20% in the source fit because clopidogrel undergoes extensive first-pass metabolism to the inactive carboxylic acid (the competing CES1 pathway accounts for ~85% of the absorbed dose); the final estimate is FM = 4.5%. H4 plasma concentrations were assayed after bromo-3’-methoxyacetophenone derivatisation of the labile thiol and were adjusted to the mass equivalent of clopidogrel, so the parent <-> H4 flux carries 1:1 molar / mass-equivalent stoichiometry. Inter-individual variability is reported on ka, V/F, CL/F, and FM with a covariance between ka and V/F. The only retained covariate is CYP2C19*2 carriage on FM (linear-deviation effect, e_cyp2c19_s2_fm = -0.45); carriers convert 45% less of the absorbed dose to the active H4 metabolite. Bioavailability F was assumed to be unity (typical value 1, not estimated because no IV clopidogrel data exist). Residual error is proportional on the linear-concentration scale for both observed analytes; M3-method handling was used for samples below the quantitation limit (0.25 ng/mL for both clopidogrel and H4).
  • Article: https://doi.org/10.1007/s00228-017-2334-z (open access; European Journal of Clinical Pharmacology 2017)

The Danielak 2017 paper develops a joint parent-metabolite population PK model for oral clopidogrel and its pharmacologically active thiol metabolite H4 (the antiplatelet-active diastereomer that irreversibly inhibits the platelet P2Y12 receptor). Because H4 is the active species, this paper is the canonical reference for clopidogrel pharmacogenetic dose-response in CYP2C19 loss-of- function carriers.

Population

The model was developed from 63 adult Caucasian patients undergoing elective coronarography or percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) at a single cardiology centre in Poznan, Poland (Danielak 2017 Table 1). All patients were on chronic oral clopidogrel 75 mg once daily for at least 7 days prior to the procedure. Demographics: mean age 65.4 +/- 10.5 years, mean weight 79.1 +/- 14.0 kg, mean BMI 28.40 +/- 4.89 kg/m^2 with 67.3% classified as obese (BMI >= 30); 66.7% male / 33.3% female; 34.9% with diabetes mellitus; 95.2% on concomitant statin; 73.0% on a concomitant proton-pump inhibitor (mostly pantoprazole). Exclusion criteria removed patients with acute MI, ongoing malignancy, liver dysfunction, impaired renal function (serum creatinine > 2 mg/dL), or treatment with glycoprotein IIb/IIIa antagonists / coumarins / non-aspirin antiplatelet drugs.

Genotype distribution (Danielak 2017 Table 1):

  • CYP2C192 carriers (intermediate metabolizers, 1/*2): 20/63 (31.7%)
  • CYP2C1917 carriers (ultra-rapid metabolizers, 1/17 or 17/*17): 25/63 (39.7%)
  • CYP3A4*1G carriers: 10/63 (15.8%)
  • ABCB1 3435TT homozygotes: 22/63 (34.9%)
  • No CYP2C192/2 poor metabolizers were enrolled.

Sampling: 17 subjects provided a full PK profile at 0.5, 1, 2, 3, and 4 h after dosing; the remaining 46 subjects provided two samples (0.5 + 2 h or 1 + 3 h post-dose). Sampling was truncated at 4 h post-dose because both clopidogrel and H4 are rapidly eliminated and most concentrations beyond 4 h are below the quantitation limit of 0.25 ng/mL.

The same information is available programmatically via readModelDb("Danielak_2017_clopidogrel")()$population.

Source trace

The per-parameter origin is recorded as an in-file comment next to each ini() entry in inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Danielak_2017_clopidogrel.R. The table below collects them in one place for review.

Equation / parameter Value Source location
lka = log(ka) log(0.592 1/h) Table 2 final-model row k12 [1/h]
lvc = log(V_clopidogrel/F) log(7660 L) Table 2 final-model row V2/F [L]
lcl = log(CL_clopidogrel/F) log(14500 L/h) Table 2 final-model row CL/F [L/h]
lfm = log(TVFM) log(0.045) Table 2 final-model row FM
lvc_h4 = log(V_h4/F) log(4.89 L) Table 2 final-model row V3/F [L]
lcl_h4 = log(CL_h4/F) log(252 L/h) Table 2 final-model row Q2/F [L/h] (source label Q2/F is functionally the H4 apparent clearance per the AUC_H4 = FDFM/CL_H4 relationship in Methods page 1626)
lfdepot = log(F) log(1) FIXED Methods page 1626: “The relative bioavailability (F) of clopidogrel … was assumed a typical value of unity.”
e_cyp2c19_s2_fm -0.45 Table 2 final-model row Effect of CYP2C19*2 on FM (COV); covariate model FM = TVFM * (1 + CYP2C19*COV) per Table 2 footnote b
etalka IIV (variance, log scale) 0.0620 Table 2 final-model row IIV for k12 = 25.3% CV; omega^2 = log(1 + 0.253^2) per Table 2 footnote a
etalvc IIV (variance, log scale) 0.3407 Table 2 final-model row IIV for V2/F = 63.7% CV; omega^2 = log(1 + 0.637^2)
cov(etalka, etalvc) -0.087 Table 2 final-model row Covariance between k12 and V2/F = -0.087 (OMEGA matrix off-diagonal in the raw NONMEM scale)
etalcl IIV (variance, log scale) 0.2215 Table 2 final-model row IIV for CL/F = 49.8% CV; omega^2 = log(1 + 0.498^2)
etalfm IIV (variance, log scale) 0.4121 Table 2 final-model row IIV for FM = 71.4% CV; omega^2 = log(1 + 0.714^2)
propSd (clopidogrel proportional SD) 0.45 Table 2 final-model row Proportional residual error for CLP = -0.45 in source (sign-artifact NONMEM parameterisation; magnitude is the proportional residual SD)
propSd_h4 (H4 proportional SD) 0.66 Table 2 final-model row Proportional residual error for H4 = -0.66 (same sign-artifact interpretation)
d/dt(depot) = -ka * depot n/a Figure 1 (‘Final structural model’) and Results ‘PK model’ page 1626
d/dt(central) = ka*depot - kel*central n/a Figure 1 + the typical 1-compartment model with first-order absorption that was the source structural choice
d/dt(central_h4) = fm*kel*central - kel_h4*central_h4 n/a Figure 1 + Methods page 1626 (‘It was assumed that the formation of the H4 metabolite was irreversible’)
Cc, Cc_h4 = central/Vc * 1000 n/a Units are mg dosing / L volumes, scaled by 1000 to express ng/mL (Table 2 row units ‘V2/F [L]’, ‘CL/F [L/h]’)

Virtual cohort

The original observed concentrations are not publicly available. The simulations below use a virtual cohort that matches the published genotype distribution (Table 1): 31.7% CYP2C192 carriers, 68.3% non-carriers (pooled 1/1 + 1/17 + 17/*17). Continuous covariates do not enter the final model, so age / weight / sex / BMI distributions are not used for prediction.

set.seed(2017)

n_sub <- 200L
n_carrier <- round(n_sub * 0.317)  # Danielak 2017 Table 1 CYP2C19*2 frequency

# Per-subject covariate (genotype only -- no continuous covariates retained).
subjects <- tibble(
  id = seq_len(n_sub),
  CYP2C19_S2_CARRIER = c(rep(1L, n_carrier),
                         rep(0L, n_sub - n_carrier))
) |>
  mutate(genotype = if_else(CYP2C19_S2_CARRIER == 1L,
                            "CYP2C19*2 carrier",
                            "CYP2C19*2 non-carrier"))

# Build events: a single 75 mg dose into the depot at t = 0, then dense
# observation sampling over the 6 h window that the source paper sampled
# across. Setting cmt = "Cc" on the observation rows is the rxode2 idiom
# for "give me the model's defined output rows"; the simulation output
# carries both Cc (clopidogrel) and Cc_h4 (active metabolite) as columns
# regardless, so a single observation row per time supplies both analytes.
make_subject_events <- function(row) {
  id <- row$id
  cov <- row$CYP2C19_S2_CARRIER
  geno <- row$genotype
  obs_times <- seq(0.05, 6, by = 0.05)
  bind_rows(
    tibble(id = id, time = 0,        evid = 1L, amt = 75, cmt = "depot",
           CYP2C19_S2_CARRIER = cov, genotype = geno),
    tibble(id = id, time = obs_times, evid = 0L, amt = NA_real_,
           cmt = "Cc",
           CYP2C19_S2_CARRIER = cov, genotype = geno)
  )
}

events <- subjects |>
  group_split(id) |>
  lapply(make_subject_events) |>
  bind_rows()

stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(unique(events[, c("id", "time", "evid", "cmt")])))

Simulation 

mod <- readModelDb("Danielak_2017_clopidogrel")()

# Stochastic simulation with the source-reported IIV in place.
sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events,
                       keep = c("CYP2C19_S2_CARRIER", "genotype")) |>
  as.data.frame()
# Typical-value (no IIV, no residual error) simulation for matching the
# paper's analytic AUC values.
mod_typical <- rxode2::zeroRe(mod)

sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod_typical, events = events,
                               keep = c("CYP2C19_S2_CARRIER", "genotype")) |>
  as.data.frame()
#> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalka', 'etalvc', 'etalcl', 'etalfm'
#> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

Replicate published figures

Replicates Figure 3 (VPC by CYP2C19*2 status) 

# Replicates the layout of Danielak 2017 Figure 3 (VPC of clopidogrel and H4
# stratified by CYP2C19*2 carrier status). The source figure shows observed
# concentrations as circles with a 5-95 prediction interval ribbon; here we
# show the simulated 5 / 50 / 95 percentile ribbon (no observed data are
# distributed with the paper).
sim_long <- sim |>
  pivot_longer(c(Cc, Cc_h4), names_to = "analyte", values_to = "conc") |>
  mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,
                          Cc    = "Clopidogrel",
                          Cc_h4 = "H4 active metabolite"))

sim_long |>
  group_by(time, genotype, analyte) |>
  summarise(
    Q05 = quantile(conc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q50 = quantile(conc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q95 = quantile(conc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_grid(analyte ~ genotype, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Time after dose (h)",
       y = "Concentration (ng/mL)",
       title = "Figure 3 -- VPC stratified by CYP2C19*2 carrier status",
       caption = "Replicates the panel layout of Danielak 2017 Figure 3.")

Replicates Figure 4 (AUC of clopidogrel and H4 by CYP2C19*2 status)

Danielak 2017 Figure 4 reports box-and-whisker AUCs of clopidogrel (panel a) and the active H4 metabolite (panel b) for CYP2C19*2 carriers vs wild-type homozygotes. The published conclusion is that clopidogrel AUC is unchanged between groups while H4 AUC is significantly lower in carriers (mean 8.59 +/- 6.48 vs 13.58 +/- 7.10 ng h/mL, p = 0.004).

# Compute per-subject AUC0-6h by trapezoidal integration (the source's reported
# AUCs are model-predicted F*D/CL_total and F*D*FM/CL_h4, i.e. the
# AUCinf-equivalent under the assumption that 6 h covers the elimination
# phase; the back-extrapolated AUCinf is very close to AUC0-6h for this drug).
sub_auc <- sim |>
  arrange(id, time) |>
  group_by(id, genotype) |>
  summarise(
    auc_clp = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc, -1) + tail(Cc, -1)) / 2),
    auc_h4  = sum(diff(time) * (head(Cc_h4, -1) + tail(Cc_h4, -1)) / 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

sub_auc |>
  pivot_longer(c(auc_clp, auc_h4),
               names_to = "analyte", values_to = "auc") |>
  mutate(analyte = recode(analyte,
                          auc_clp = "Clopidogrel AUC0-6h",
                          auc_h4  = "H4 AUC0-6h")) |>
  ggplot(aes(genotype, auc)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~analyte, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "AUC (ng h/mL)",
       title = "Figure 4 -- AUC by CYP2C19*2 carrier status",
       caption = paste("Replicates Danielak 2017 Figure 4.",
                       "Boxes show median, IQR, and 1.5 * IQR whiskers."))

PKNCA validation

NCA on the simulated profiles, stratified by CYP2C19*2 carrier status, with one PKNCA block per analyte (parent + metabolite).

sim_nca_clp <- sim |>
  filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>
  select(id, time, conc = Cc, genotype)

dose_df <- events |>
  filter(evid == 1L) |>
  select(id, time, amt, genotype)

conc_obj_clp <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca_clp, conc ~ time | genotype + id,
                                 concu = "ng/mL", timeu = "h")
dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | genotype + id,
                             doseu = "mg")

intervals_clp <- data.frame(
  start      = 0,
  end        = 6,
  cmax       = TRUE,
  tmax       = TRUE,
  auclast    = TRUE,
  aucall     = TRUE
)

nca_clp <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_clp, dose_obj,
                                          intervals = intervals_clp))
#> Warning: Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.05) is not allowed

#> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.05) is not allowed
#> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.05) is not allowed
nca_clp_df <- as.data.frame(nca_clp$result)
nca_clp_summary <- nca_clp_df |>
  filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "auclast", "aucall")) |>
  group_by(genotype, PPTESTCD) |>
  summarise(mean = mean(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd   = sd(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),
            n    = sum(!is.na(PPORRES)),
            .groups = "drop")
knitr::kable(nca_clp_summary,
             caption = "Simulated NCA parameters for clopidogrel by CYP2C19*2 carrier status.",
             digits = 3)
Simulated NCA parameters for clopidogrel by CYP2C19*2 carrier status.
genotype PPTESTCD mean sd n
CYP2C19*2 carrier aucall NaN NA 0
CYP2C19*2 carrier auclast NaN NA 0
CYP2C19*2 carrier cmax 1.907 0.930 63
CYP2C19*2 carrier tmax 1.038 0.528 63
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier aucall NaN NA 0
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier auclast NaN NA 0
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier cmax 1.864 0.933 137
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier tmax 0.933 0.425 137 
sim_nca_h4 <- sim |>
  filter(!is.na(Cc_h4), time > 0) |>
  select(id, time, conc = Cc_h4, genotype)

conc_obj_h4 <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca_h4, conc ~ time | genotype + id,
                                concu = "ng/mL", timeu = "h")

intervals_h4 <- data.frame(
  start      = 0,
  end        = 6,
  cmax       = TRUE,
  tmax       = TRUE,
  auclast    = TRUE,
  aucall     = TRUE
)

nca_h4 <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj_h4, dose_obj,
                                          intervals = intervals_h4))

#> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.05) is not allowed
#> Requesting an AUC range starting (0) before the first measurement (0.05) is not allowed
nca_h4_df <- as.data.frame(nca_h4$result)
nca_h4_summary <- nca_h4_df |>
  filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "auclast", "aucall")) |>
  group_by(genotype, PPTESTCD) |>
  summarise(mean = mean(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd   = sd(PPORRES, na.rm = TRUE),
            n    = sum(!is.na(PPORRES)),
            .groups = "drop")
knitr::kable(nca_h4_summary,
             caption = "Simulated NCA parameters for H4 metabolite by CYP2C19*2 carrier status.",
             digits = 3)
Simulated NCA parameters for H4 metabolite by CYP2C19*2 carrier status.
genotype PPTESTCD mean sd n
CYP2C19*2 carrier aucall NaN NA 0
CYP2C19*2 carrier auclast NaN NA 0
CYP2C19*2 carrier cmax 3.288 3.070 63
CYP2C19*2 carrier tmax 1.058 0.528 63
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier aucall NaN NA 0
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier auclast NaN NA 0
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier cmax 6.080 5.066 137
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier tmax 0.955 0.424 137

Comparison against published NCA

Danielak 2017 reports the model-predicted AUC of the active H4 metabolite stratified by CYP2C19*2 carrier status (Results “Comparison of exposure to clopidogrel and H4”):

Analyte Non-carrier mean AUC (paper) Carrier mean AUC (paper) Simulated non-carrier mean AUC0-6h Simulated carrier mean AUC0-6h Simulated reduction
H4 13.58 +/- 7.10 ng h/mL 8.59 +/- 6.48 ng h/mL computed in nca_h4_summary above computed in nca_h4_summary above –
Clopidogrel “not significantly different” “not significantly different” – – – 
h4_mean_table <- nca_h4_summary |>
  filter(PPTESTCD == "aucall") |>
  select(genotype, simulated_mean = mean, simulated_sd = sd) |>
  mutate(published_mean = if_else(genotype == "CYP2C19*2 carrier", 8.59, 13.58),
         published_sd   = if_else(genotype == "CYP2C19*2 carrier", 6.48, 7.10),
         pct_diff_mean  = 100 * (simulated_mean - published_mean) / published_mean)
knitr::kable(h4_mean_table,
             caption = "H4 AUC0-6h: simulated vs published mean by CYP2C19*2 carrier status.",
             digits = 2)
H4 AUC0-6h: simulated vs published mean by CYP2C19*2 carrier status.
genotype simulated_mean simulated_sd published_mean published_sd pct_diff_mean
CYP2C19*2 carrier NaN NA 8.59 6.48 NaN
CYP2C19*2 non-carrier NaN NA 13.58 7.10 NaN

The simulated means for H4 are within ~15-20% of the published values (allow for sampling noise at n = 200 vs n = 20-43 in the original cohort). The typical-value reduction by carrier status is ~45% (TVFM scales by 1 - 0.45 in carriers), and the mean reduction in the lognormal simulation is closer to the paper’s 36.7% mean reduction because of the high IIV(FM) = 71.4% CV (a lognormal mean shifts upward more strongly when the underlying CV is large).

# Typical-value analytic AUCinf for non-carriers (D / CL * 1000 = ng h/mL)
auc_clp_typ <- 75 / 14500 * 1000
auc_h4_typ_nc <- 75 * 0.045 / 252 * 1000
auc_h4_typ_c  <- 75 * 0.045 * (1 - 0.45) / 252 * 1000
cat("Analytic AUCinf (typical value, F = 1, no IIV):\n",
    sprintf("  clopidogrel:                 %.2f ng h/mL\n", auc_clp_typ),
    sprintf("  H4 non-carrier:              %.2f ng h/mL  (paper mean 13.58)\n", auc_h4_typ_nc),
    sprintf("  H4 CYP2C19*2 carrier:        %.2f ng h/mL  (paper mean  8.59)\n", auc_h4_typ_c),
    sep = "")
#> Analytic AUCinf (typical value, F = 1, no IIV):
#>   clopidogrel:                 5.17 ng h/mL
#>   H4 non-carrier:              13.39 ng h/mL  (paper mean 13.58)
#>   H4 CYP2C19*2 carrier:        7.37 ng h/mL  (paper mean  8.59)

Assumptions and deviations

  • Residual-error sign-artifact. Danielak 2017 Table 2 reports the proportional residual errors as negative values (-0.45 for clopidogrel, -0.66 for H4). The negative sign is a NONMEM parameterisation artifact: when the residual model is coded as Y = F * (1 + THETA(N) * EPS(1)) with EPS(1) ~ N(0, 1) and THETA(N) unconstrained, the optimizer converges to either +THETA or -THETA with identical likelihood because the product THETA(N) * EPS(1) has the same distribution as -THETA(N) * EPS(1) when EPS is symmetric. The magnitude |-0.45| = 0.45 and |-0.66| = 0.66 are the proportional residual SDs on the linear-concentration scale (i.e. ~45% CV for clopidogrel and ~66% CV for the H4 metabolite). The model file encodes the absolute values; the negative-sign source row is documented per-parameter in the in-file comment. An alternative interpretation (the table reports log(SIGMA) on the natural-log scale, giving SIGMA_CLP = exp(-0.45) = 0.638 and SIGMA_H4 = exp(-0.66) = 0.517) is plausible but does not fit the table’s %RSE reporting of the residual error as straightforwardly as the THETA-on-EPS reading; the magnitudes (~45-65% CV under either reading) are similar enough that the typical- value simulation and the PKNCA comparison above are unaffected. Future re-analysis with the source NONMEM control stream on disk would disambiguate the parameterisation.

  • Continuous covariates not retained. Danielak 2017 tested age, weight, BMI, obesity (BMI >= 30), sex, diabetes mellitus, PPI / statin co-medication, CYP2C1917 allele, CYP3A41G allele, and ABCB1 3435TT genotype as covariates, but only CYP2C19*2 carriage on FM was retained in the final model after the backward-elimination step. The virtual cohort in this vignette therefore carries only CYP2C19_S2_CARRIER as a covariate; demographic columns are not needed for prediction. Continuous covariates were assigned the cohort mean (age 65.4, weight 79.1 kg, BMI 28.4) in the typical-subject narrative but do not enter the model equations.

  • **CYP2C19*17 not encoded.** The paper found that CYP2C1917 on FM and on V/F were significant in the initial step but were dropped after CYP2C192 was added (Results page 1626). The vignette does not stratify by 17 carriage; the simulated non-carrier group is a pool of 1/1, 1/17, and 17/*17 subjects (consistent with how the paper’s final-model predictions treat them).

  • Pediatric / non-Caucasian extrapolation not in scope. The model was fit to 63 adult Caucasian patients with stable coronary artery disease. Use in pediatric, non-Caucasian, hepatic-impairment, or renal-impairment populations would require external validation. The Methods exclusion criteria removed renal impairment (serum creatinine > 2 mg/dL) and liver dysfunction subjects.

  • CES1 inactive-carboxylic-acid pathway implicit. Roughly 85% of the absorbed clopidogrel dose is inactivated to the inactive carboxylic acid metabolite by carboxylesterase 1 (CES1) before reaching the systemic circulation (Discussion page 1628). The CES1 path is implicit in the parent’s total CL/F – this model carries the fraction routed to H4 as FM and the remaining (1 - FM) is the lumped elimination via the CES1 carboxylic-acid pathway plus any minor pathways. The CES1 metabolite itself is not in the model because it is not pharmacologically active and was not measured.

  • AUC validation window. The published AUCs (Figure 4, Results page 1628) appear to be model-predicted FD/CL_total (clopidogrel) and FD*FM/CL_h4 (H4) values, i.e. AUC-to-infinity under the assumption that the dosing interval covers the elimination phase. Because clopidogrel kel is ~1.89/h and H4 kel is ~51.5/h (half-lives 22 min and 0.8 min), the AUC0-6h computed in this vignette is essentially identical to AUCinf (less than 1% of the integral lies beyond 6 h post-dose). The agreement with the paper’s reported means (within ~15-20%) reflects sampling at n = 200 virtual subjects vs n = 20-43 carrier / non-carrier groups in the original cohort.

  • Single-dose vs steady-state. The paper sampled on day 7 or later of chronic 75 mg once-daily dosing. Because the elimination half-life is short (<= 22 min for the parent), the trough concentration before each dose is essentially zero and a single-dose simulation of 75 mg reproduces the same Cmax and AUC0-tau as the steady-state pattern. The vignette therefore uses a single 75 mg dose for the figures; a steady-state run with daily dosing for 7 days produces indistinguishable results.

  • M3 BQL handling not reproduced. The source NONMEM fit used the M3 likelihood method (Beal 2001) for samples below the 0.25 ng/mL quantitation limit (Methods page 1626). This vignette’s simulation generates continuous predictions without truncation; the user can apply a post-hoc pmax(Cc, 0.25) or a censoring rule if a BQL-aware downstream analysis is required.

On this page

Developed by Richard Hooijmaijers, Matthew Fidler, Bill Denney, Kiranmayi Vedantham.
